About Me
Hi, I am Saksham, a PhD student in Civil Engineering at the University of Wyoming. Originally from Sindhupalchowk, Nepal, my research focuses on energy infrastructure safety, 3D laser scanning, and data-driven risk modeling. Outside the lab, I am a passionate football fan and closely follow the Premier League and Champions League.
Research Interests
Energy Infrastructure Safety and Resilience Construction Disputes and Resolution Laser Scanning / LiDAR Machine Learning Hydrogen Energy H2 Transportation Infrastructure Nuclear Energy Construction Safety
Current Position
PhD Student · Graduate Research and Teaching Assistant
University of Wyoming · Dept. of Civil and Architectural Engineering and Construction Management
Advised by Prof. Chengyi Zhang. Working on energy infrastructure safety and risk mapping, 3D laser scanning for infrastructure inspection, regulatory landscape, and automation framework for infrastructure safety. Expected graduation: May 2027.
Energy InfrastructureLaser ScanningRisk MappingML Automation

Research
Research Areas
Hydrogen Transportation Infrastructure Optimization
Wyoming Case Studies · 2024–2025
Studying feasibility and optimization of hydrogen transport across Wyoming, including blending into natural gas pipelines, trucking logistics, and supply chain optimization under uncertainty using Mixed Integer Linear Programming and Real Options Analysis.
MILPReal OptionsSupply ChainWyoming
Hydrogen Pipeline Safety and Dispute Resolution
ML-Based Prediction · 2024–2025
Analysis of environmental, safety, regulatory, and legal challenges in hydrogen pipeline infrastructure. Developed ML-based predictive models to forecast dispute occurrence and recommend resolution strategies in pipeline projects.
Machine LearningDispute PredictionRisk Analysis
Hydrogen Infrastructure Reliability and Incident Analysis
Uncertainty Quantification · 2025
Integrated framework using historical incident data to quantify reliability and uncertainty in hydrogen infrastructure systems. Published in Journal of Loss Prevention in the Process Industries (2026).
Reliability AnalysisIncident DataUncertainty
Regulatory and Policy Frameworks for H2 Pipelines
Comparative Analysis · 2025
Comparative analysis of regulatory and policy frameworks governing hydrogen pipeline development across jurisdictions, identifying gaps and pathways for harmonization.
Policy AnalysisRegulatory Compliance
Data-Driven Energy Infrastructure Monitoring and Safety WIP
Laser Scanning and ML · In Progress
Investigating transmission powerline safety and structural resilience using advanced laser scanning and predictive machine learning models. Assessing vegetation encroachment risk, structural degradation, and grid reliability in wildfire-prone regions of Wyoming and Colorado.
LiDARPowerline SafetyWildfire RiskGrid Resilience
3D Laser Scanning for Infrastructure Inspection and Preservation WIP
Terrestrial and Aerial LiDAR · 2024–Present
Application of terrestrial and UAV-mounted laser scanners for 3D modeling, inspection, and maintenance of civil and heritage infrastructure. Active projects include Laramie Railroad Depot, Fort Laramie, Cheyenne High Plains Arboretum, and Cheyenne Capitol Building.
LiDARUAVPoint CloudHeritage Preservation
Post-Wildfire Infrastructure Risk Assessment WIP
3D Spatial Analytics · In Progress
ML-driven 3D spatial analytics for post-wildfire infrastructure and landscape risk assessment using laser scanning, targeting critical infrastructure in wildfire-affected areas of Wyoming and Colorado.
Post-WildfireSpatial AnalyticsMLLiDAR
Digital Preservation of Heritage Infrastructure WIP
Medicine Bow and UW Apatosaurus · In Progress
Digital preservation of at-risk heritage structures using laser scanning and photogrammetry for long-term archival, covering structures in the Medicine Bow National Forest and the Apatosaurus fossil collection at UW.
Heritage PreservationDigital TwinPhotogrammetry
Small Modular Nuclear Reactor (SMR) Deployment
Regulatory and Legal Analysis · 2025
Comparative analysis of regulatory compliance and legal challenges in deploying small modular nuclear reactors, exploring pathways for accelerated licensing and dispute resolution.
Nuclear EnergySMRLegal Compliance
Construction Safety and Workplace Accident Prediction
Gender-Centric Fatal Accident Analysis · 2024
Multi-output regression models to predict gender-differentiated fatal workplace accidents in construction, identifying high-risk scenarios for evidence-based safety policy.
Construction SafetyRegression MLFatal Accidents
Flood Inundation and Dam Breach Analysis
Mugu-Karnali Basin, Nepal · 2021–2022
Undergraduate research using GIS and HEC-RAS to model flood inundation extents and dam breach scenarios in a high-gradient river basin for disaster risk assessment.
HEC-RASGISDisaster Risk
